Entries.Name: Narrator
Entries.Line: Being a Kweebec, particularly that from France, he most likely knows the Emerald Wilds better than you do. You're not sure what news you could possibly bring to him -- or, indeed, whether he would put up with you retelling all of his ancestral knowledge for the sake of conversation.

Entries.Name: Narrator
Entries.Line: Either way, you give it a shot. You tell him of the mountains that surround your home within a vaguely heart-shaped valley: a feature that seems weirdly common for this kind of biome, from what you've heard.

Entries.Name: French Kweebec
Entries.Line: If this was in France, you would not have any peace from les touristes !

Entries.Name: Narrator
Entries.Line: You led a simple life in comparison to some of the more advanced human societies. There was no bureaucracy, no industry -- just you, your community, and your cozy little farm.

Entries.Name: Narrator
Entries.Line: But there was nothing else to do. Sure, you didn't feel that way as a child, but there seemed to be an unspoken law that your youthful antics are something to 'grow out of' once you reach adulthood.

Entries.Name: Narrator
Entries.Line: What then? Humans are not built to stay as idle as a Kweebec Ancient, even if your grandparents' long excursions to the couch were trying to prove otherwise. And there was only so much farming you can do with the four-five crops that existed in your vicinity.

Line.Text: There was but a single option in your eyes. Go explore the rest of the little world you've seen from your village. If you ever come back to farming, you'll definitely have a more exotic offering of produce.
